task_completion.description: Navigate to or open the physics engine workspace directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, IDE, or terminal showing the directory /home/ga/workspace/physics_engine/.

task_completion.description: Run the test suite initially to identify the failing tests caused by the planted bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing test execution (e.g., pytest or unittest) with failing tests (e.g., FAILED, AssertionError) related to physics or math.

task_completion.description: Open the engine source files to locate the physics and math bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing files from the 'engine/' directory (such as vector2d.py, integrator.py, or collision.py) open in the workspace.

task_completion.description: Edit the physics engine code to fix the bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing active modifications, unsaved changes, or terminal showing git diffs indicating changes to the math/physics logic in the 'engine/' directory.

task_completion.description: Run the test suite successfully after fixing the bugs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the test suite running and passing completely (e.g., 'OK', 'passed' in green text) with no test failures.

integrity.description: Verify the test suite was genuinely executed and not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows standard test runner output (like pytest or unittest) with execution time, collected items, and test counts, rather than a simple echoed 'Success' string.

integrity.description: Verify the test files were not modified or deleted to force a pass.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code editor, file explorer, or git status does not show modifications to the test files themselves; changes should be restricted to the 'engine/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the engine code rather than bypassing the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible changes or saved modifications in the engine/*.py files (like vector2d.py, integrator.py, collision.py) addressing math/physics logic.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The workspace is located at /home/ga/workspace/physics_engine/. The bugs are planted in the 'engine/' directory, specifically in modules like vector2d.py, integrator.py, and collision.py. The agent must fix 5 bugs related to vector math, numerical integration, and collision detection. The test suite must pass to complete the task. The underlying verifier requires a score of at least 60 to pass.
